excluded_defs.name: determineFilesystemAndPath
excluded_defs.reason: read: understanding the separator check (strpos < 1) is the same condition used here to detect a root-level location

exploration_log.aha_moment: determineFilesystemAndPath already uses `strpos($path, '://') < 1` to throw on missing separator — the inverse of this same check is exactly the condition to detect a root/cross-mount listing request.

alternatives: Use the same strpos check as determineFilesystemAndPath
key_decisions.decision: Use strpos($location, '://') < 1 to detect any location without a mount prefix
key_decisions.reasoning: This exactly mirrors the existing separator check and handles all root-level inputs (empty string, '/', paths without scheme)

solve_notes: Added a branch at the top of `listContents` that detects when the location has no `://` separator (indicating a root-level request). In this case, the method iterates all registered filesystems, maps each listing to prepend the mount identifier prefix, and returns a merged `DirectoryListing` backed by a generator that yields from all mounts.
task_text:
Fix MountManager.listContents not supporting deep listing across mounts

In `src/MountManager.php`, `listContents(string $location, bool $deep)`
resolves a single filesystem for the location and delegates the
listing. When called with a root-level path and `deep = true`, it
does not aggregate listings across all mounted filesystems. Fix
`listContents` to iterate over all mounts when the location is the
root or matches multiple mount points, merging results with mount
prefix prepended.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: determineFilesystemAndPath
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the separator check `strpos($path, '://') < 1` in this method is reused verbatim as the root-detection condition; not seeing it would cause the agent to invent an inconsistent check

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the edited method, the determineFilesystemAndPath separator check, and the DirectoryListing constructor. An AI agent would also specifically look at determineFilesystemAndPath to understand the exact separator check expression to reuse; this prevents the agent from using a different condition like `empty($location)` that would miss other root-level inputs.
